params.name: Bull-Leaping Fresco
params.description:
On Mar 22, 1899 AD, archaeologists excavated the Bull-Leaping Fresco in the Palace of Knossos.
This ancient mural was created during the Minoan civilization on the island of Crete, depicting a scene of three young men and women completing a rite of passage. In the painting, a fair-skinned woman on the left grasps the bull's horns while a brown-skinned man vaults over the bull's back, conveying a strong sense of movement. The emergence of this mural demonstrates the high level of artistic achi …
